verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 (inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor type 3) is a multi-pass TM Ca2+ release channel canonically resident on the ER membrane, with C-terminal/N-terminal domains facing cytoplasm and the channel pore releasing Ca2+ from ER lumen to cytosol. However, there is documented plasma-membrane localization of IP3R3 in several cell types (e.g., apical PM of polarized epithelial cells, B cells, and immune cells), where it can mediate Ca2+ influx and has been detected by surface biotinylation/immunostaining on intact cells. This qualifies as contextual via dual_localization — minority PM pool alongside dominant ER residency, with its own multi-pass TM anchor at the PM.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Uncertain whether PM pool of IP3R3 is robust/generalizable enough vs. predominantly ER-resident; surface evidence is cell-type-restricted and not universally accepted.
